description_long: This bungalow features a distinctive cross-shaped footprint with a prominent, multi-faceted red-tiled hip roof that extends outward in four directions. The roof is steeply pitched and visually dominates the structure, with deep overhangs providing shade to the perimeter. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden slats, alternating between dark and light tones, creating a striped effect. The open lower sections suggest the presence of wide, covered verandas or porches on all sides, supported by visible beams and posts. There are no visible windows or doors, but the open design implies easy access and a focus on outdoor living. The overall appearance is both striking and functional, blending traditional bungalow elements with a unique geometric layout.
description: A cross-shaped bungalow with a large, red-tiled hip roof and striped wooden exterior, featuring deep overhangs and open verandas on all sides.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central roof peak and a wide, open veranda with striped wooden supports beneath the overhang.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the roof overhang and the open veranda area, with visible beams and slatted supports.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the symmetrical cross-shaped roof and the continuation of the striped wooden exterior, with open lower sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the same deep roof overhang and open veranda, with clear visibility of the supporting beams and slats.
